#### "Recognized Leader in Data Center Intelligent PDU Solutions"

Digipower Manufacturing Inc. is recognized as a key vendor in the global data center Rack PDU market, as identified by leading international market research institutions including Technavio and Introspective Market Research.

Founded in 1999, Digipower has become a recognized leader in intelligent power distribution and remote power management solutions for critical data center environments. Based in Taiwan, our extensive PDU portfolio, including advanced per-outlet managed PDUs, supports global OEM partners and infrastructure operators in optimizing power efficiency, reducing downtime, and scaling operations. By combining OEM/ODM design expertise with rigorous quality assurance, Digipower continues to advance data center innovations.

### DATA CENTER PDU MARKET SIZE, SHARE & TRENDS

According to Marketsandmarkets, the data center PDU market size was valued USD 3.94 biliion in 2025 and is project to reach USD 14.23 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 19.5% from 2026 to 2032. The data center PDU market is driven by the expansion of hyperscale and AI-driven data centers, the increasing rack power density, and the rising adoption of intelligent PDUs for real-time monitoring and operational efficiency. Additionally, the strong focus on energy efficiency, sustainability, and reliable power distribution infrastructure is further supporting the demand for advanced PDU solutions across modern data center environments.

### MARKET SNAPSHOT TABLE

- Eaton (Ireland)
- Vertiv Group Corp. (US)

- ABB (Switzerland)

### Case study

#### Background

Greenergy builds energy-efficient data centers using Estonia’s low carbon energy sources and cold climate to power and cool its facilities. Over time, its vision is to expand operations across the Baltics, harnessing alternative energy sources and proving that high-performance data centers can operate responsibly. Now Estonia’s leading colocation provider, Greenergy targets HPC and AI customers with IT loads exceeding 10 MW, prompting upgrades to its power and liquid cooling infrastructure. Supporting this evolution is A-Kaabel OÜ, an established technology company in Estonia delivering comprehensive design, construction, and maintenance of technical systems, 24/7 infrastructure support and monitoring services across Europe. Since 2020, A-Kaabel has been an authorized sales and service partner for Vertiv in Estonia.

##### Project milestones

• New facility opening: Built and opened a new facility in late 2022.

• Ramp-up of remaining capacity: Ramping up and consuming the remaining capacity based on increasing AI and HPC capacity demands. 
• Future expansion: Well-positioned to promptly react and build out to meet further customer demands.

## Monitored PDUs for Serbian Telecom Leader A Vertiv Case Study

### Background

The telecommunications industry in Serbia is competitive, with several major players offering a wide range of services, such as mobile, fixed-line, and broadband internet. Companies within this sector focus on providing reliable connectivity solutions to residential and business customers, driving digital transformation and connectivity advancements in Serbia.

### Challenge

Facing urgent infrastructure needs after the COVID-19 pandemic, a leading Serbian telecom company was searching for a server rack solution with fast delivery and monitored PDUs (power distribution units) compatible with their existing DCIM (Data Center Infrastructure Management) solution. The urgency stemmed from the need to optimize infrastructure deployment timelines while providing seamless integration with established monitoring and management systems. By prioritizing compatibility and expedited delivery, the customer aimed to streamline operations, enhance resource utilization, and maintain the reliability and efficiency of their data center environment.

# Breaking Vendor Lock-In for a European Bank

### How a major high-street bank transitioned PDU vendors without losing control or visibility

When a leading European bank decided to change its intelligent PDU supplier, the challenge extended far beyond hardware replacement. Furthermore, the bank operated multiple data centre sites and relied on consistent power monitoring and strict rack-level security. Any disruption or fragmentation in management tools was not an option.

Undoubtedly the priority was clear: move to a new PDU vendor while maintaining a single, unified control platform.

REQUEST CASE STUDY

Valid corporate email address

## The Challenge: Change Without Complexity

The bank needed to replace its existing intelligent PDUs after a vendor partnership ended. However, removing all legacy hardware at once was neither practical nor desirable.

Furthermore, running two separate management systems in parallel would increase operational risk, slow response times, and add unnecessary complexity. At the same time, the bank required fully managed rack access control across all sites, with clear governance, auditing, and accountability.

The solution had to support both legacy and new PDUs simultaneously, while keeping power monitoring, environmental data, and security workflows under one roof.

IND Park is an integrated production, technology and logistics hub for the rail, high tech and aerospace industry. Technosector has been a key MIND Park partner since the original plan to develop the greenfield site into a technology park and logistics hub started with the construction of a single building.      "text": "Case study PDU 5.0 en EnerTree DCEM Software

# Case study PDU 5.0

The Schleifenbauer PDU 5.0 wasn’t conceived in a lab, but shaped through conversations with over 40 data centre managers across Europe. Their needs and insights laid the foundation for a smart, scalable and fully customisable PDU solution — including licence-free monitoring software. This case study explains how that collaboration resulted in a powerful, future-proof solution for today’s data centres, particularly in AI and HPC environments.

How 40 Data Centre Managers Helped Shape the Schleifenbauer PDU 5.0

The Challenge: Traditional PDUs No Longer Meet Modern Demands

Data centres are evolving fast. Workloads are increasing in complexity, energy efficiency is under greater scrutiny, and infrastructure must adapt to the rising pressure of growth. With the advent of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and High Performance Computing (HPC), power distribution must go beyond simple current delivery.

What we heard from over 40 European data centre managers was remarkably consistent:

• “We need real-time visibility into power usage and load distribution.”

• “Maintenance should be predictive, based on data—not reactive.”

• “We’re tired of high licence fees for energy monitoring tools.”

• “Don’t make us adapt the rack to the PDU—adapt the PDU to the rack.”

These insights sparked the development of a fundamentally different approach to power distribution. The result? The PDU 5.0 and licence-free EnerTree DCEM softwa"

Power distribution is a critical factor in any data centre. DPI’s clients required high-quality, flexible, and efficient PDUs that could seamlessly integrate into existing infrastructures. Standard PDUs often lack the necessary customisation and scalability needed for modern, high-performance data centres.

DPI needed a PDU partner that could provide reliable, tailored solutions without long lead times—ensuring seamless integration and future-proof performance.
